memoryArtifacts.description: He married Sybil Vessey on December 10, 1561, in Little Baddow, Essex, England. They had 15 children in 28 years. John Porter was born on June 12, 1576, in Foxearth, Essex, England. He died on June 20 …

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Porter於1563年2月21日出生在英國時，他的父親Thomas Porter I22歲，母親Anne Marie Calvert21歲。他於1587年9月15日與Sybil Vessey在英國艾可蕾托 巴多福結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1625年被列為yeoman。他去世於1625年11月26日在他的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦霍爾波恩。

nameMeanings.name: Porter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porterfield
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English porter(e) ‘gatekeeper, doorkeeper’ + feld ‘open country, bounded piece of land’, referring to the hereditary lands owned by a porter of a monastery (see Porter 1), taken as a surname by the descendants of such an official. Black suggests that they would originally have been known as Porter but extended their name when ‘territorial surnames’ became fashionable.

variants.name: Sluiter
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a porter or gatekeeper, Middle Dutch sluter. Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Port
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Fortner
meanings: German: variant (also Förtner) or Americanized form of Pförtner, an occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper in a great house, castle, or monastery, Middle High German phortenaere (from Latin porta ‘door, gate’). Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Portier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clovis, Leonce, Renauld.
French: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, Old French portier (from Late Latin portarius). Compare English Porter .
History: Alexandre-Bernard Portier from Paris, France, married Catherine Monte in Edgard, LA, in 1741.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When John Porter was born on 21 February 1563, in England, his father, Thomas Porter I, was 22 and his mother, Anne Marie Calvert, was 21. He married Sybil Vessey on 15 September 1587, in Little Baddow, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. In 1625, at the age of 62, his occupation is listed as yeoman. He died on 26 November 1625, in his hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Holborn, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Porter est né le 21 février 1563, à Angleterre, son père, Thomas Porter I, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Anne Marie Calvert, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Sybil Vessey le 15 septembre 1587, à Little Baddow, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. En 1625, à l’âge de 62 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est yeoman. Il est décédé le 26 novembre 1625, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Holborn, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Little Baddow, Chelmsford, Essex, Englan: Little Baddow, Essex, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als John Porter am 21. Februar 1563 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Porter I 22 und seine Mutter Anne Marie Calvert 21. Er hat am 15. September 1587 in Little Baddow, Essex, England Sybil Vessey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1625, als er 62 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als yeoman aufgeführt. Er starb am 26. November 1625 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Holborn, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Sybil Vessey il 15 settembre 1587, nel Little Baddow, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Nel 1625, all'età di 62 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come yeoman. Morì il 26 novembre 1625, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Holborn, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Porterが1563年2月21日にイギリスで生まれた時，父Thomas Porter Iは22歳，母Anne Marie Calvertは21歳であった。1587年9月15日にイギリスエセックスリットル バッドオウでSybil Vesseyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1625年，62歳の時，職業はyeomanと記録されている。彼は1625年11月26日に故郷で62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンホールボルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Porter(이)가 1563년 2월 21일 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Porter I(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Marie Calvert(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1587년 9월 15일 영국 에세스 리틀 바도에서 Sybil Vessey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1625년, 62세의 나이로 그의 직업은 yeoman이었다. 그는 1625년 11월 26일 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 홀본에 묻혔다.

pt.Little Baddow, Chelmsford, Essex, Englan: Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Porter nasceu em 21 de fevereiro de 1563, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Porter I, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Anne Marie Calvert, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Sybil Vessey em 15 de setembro de 1587, em Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Em 1625, com 62 anos, sua profissão aparece como yeoman. Ele faleceu em 26 de novembro de 1625, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Holborn, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ro.Little Baddow, Chelmsford, Essex, Englan: Little Baddow, Essex, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Porter родился 21 февраля 1563 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Porter I было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Marie Calvert, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Sybil Vessey 15 сентября 1587 г. в местности литл баддоу, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. В 1625 году, в возрасте 62 лет,  он работал по профессии yeoman. Он умер 26 ноября 1625 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности холборн, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Porter nació el 21 de febrero de 1563, en Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Porter I, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Anne Marie Calvert, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sybil Vessey el 15 de septiembre de 1587, en Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. En 1625, a la edad de 62 años, su ocupación registrada era yeoman. Murió el 26 de noviembre de 1625, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Holborn, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
